group.name: Loop-policy residue after the criteria-anchor landing

statement: CLAUDE.md line 100's Autonomous-loop authorization paragraph stands at 258aeac0 as a single ~200-word run-on line the user challenged as unreadable on 2026-07-18 and the assistant ratified as not clear — the in-window fix (82500702) removed only the two cap clauses, no clarity change landed, and no substrate item tracks the defect
action: Surface to the user as a scope call, then execute only under direction — the session record has the assistant committed to not rewriting the binding paragraph without the user's direction, and the user rejected having his decision bounded for him. Under direction: restructure the one-line paragraph into readable declarative rules preserving its semantic content exactly (mandate-008 applicability, loop non-stop, fresh-agent re-dispatch condition, deterministic gate, user-surfacing triggers) with no added narration and no register violations; if the user wants the defect tracked rather than fixed now, file it as a substrate item. Distinct from DW-27's cap-clause residue: the cap deletion landed in-window (82500702); this is the surviving readability defect of the same paragraph.

out: rewriting before the user directs it

verify: Either the paragraph is restructured into a form the user ratifies on the record (semantics unchanged, no single-line run-on), or the user explicitly declines/re-sequences the rewrite, or a substrate item tracks the defect; at present none of the three holds.

history.note: new-window discovery: window commits 6867b011/82500702/258aeac0 and session c41e910c 2026-07-17..19; surfaced as the sole still-open of both in-window /session-audit runs (DA-01 in the first, re-anchored to user verbatim in the second); other in-window open items map onto DW-20..DW-29
